items.title: 'Intolerable': When $786m bridge was being designed, engineers raised a red flag

children.content: Despite government assurances Hobart's Bridgewater Bridge is safe, there are fresh concerns over its foundation design, with one expert who worked on the project suggesting it could eventually lead to a need to reduce traffic flow or speed limits.

gallery.altText: A silver metal device attached to a concrete pylon of a bridge.
Close up of reinforcing measures in a bridge structure.
An close up of a bridge design drawing, with an engineer pointing to the piles.

thumbnail.altText: A silver metal device attached to a concrete pylon of a bridge.
Close up of reinforcing measures in a bridge structure.
An close up of a bridge design drawing, with an engineer pointing to the piles.

items.title: Notorious children's jail could become minimum-security adult prison farm

image.altText: An aerial view of Ashley Youth Detention Centre.

children.content: The Tasmanian government says Ashley Youth Detention Centre could become a minimum-security adult prison and "agriculture-based prison rehab and training facility".

items.title: Youth escapee avoids recording conviction for knifepoint carjacking of tourists

children.content: One of the teenagers involved in a group escape from the Ashley Youth Detention Centre in December is sentenced for his involvement in the armed robbery of two tourists.

image.altText: A coastal town along a beach with yellow sand under a partly cloudy sky seen from above.

items.title: Tasmania 'just wanted to roll trucks in' as quick fix for icebreaker refuelling saga

children.content: A federal department says the Tasmanian government's proposed interim solution to a costly refuelling saga affecting Australia's Antarctic icebreaker is "far from" acceptable.

image.altText: The red-hulled Nuyina next to the dock in Hobart.

items.title: Tasmania spent $47m on digital program that didn't deliver any modules

children.content: The total cost of the digital transformation program is now estimated to more than double to $119 million, the auditor-general warns.

image.altText: Medical staffer using laptop with stethoscope nearby.

items.title: Mobile GP service and after-hours care to end because of Tasmanian health cuts

children.content: Primary care providers being forced to cut services after losing Tasmanian government funding say the result will be added strain on "pressure cooker" emergency departments, which could see them "explode".

image.altText: A doctor using a stethoscope on a patient.

items.title: Labor to ignore call for pause on CGT changes

children.content: The government is pushing ahead with capital gains tax changes while consulting on limited carve-outs.

image.altText: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in a grey suit, staring to his right, with the Australian flag behind him.

images.altText: Officers usher away a protester in parliament house.

articles.title: Israeli politician calls for investigation after Gaza flotilla activists allege abuse

children.content: One of Israel's most prominent opposition politicians has called for an investigation into the alleged abuse of activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla, which was intercepted by the Israeli military in the Mediterranean last week.

image.altText: Yair Lapid speaks at a lectern gesturing with one hand.

articles.title: Labor to ignore call for pause on CGT changes

children.content: The government is pushing ahead with capital gains tax changes while consulting on limited carve-outs.

articles.title: Government sets up fight with Santos over gas reservation plan

children.content: The draft framework outlines a requirement for gas producers to supply 20 per cent of their export to the domestic market, which the gas lobby argues will "undermine" the industry.

image.altText: A woman in a blue jacket looking pensive.

children.content: Activists stage a protest at Parliament House in Canberra against Israel's treatment of Gaza flotilla activists. Follow live.

items.title: After losing her 3yo daughter, one mum says MDMA therapy saved her life

image.altText: Woman with short brown hair looks at camera in a green silk shirt and in a well lit therapy room.

children.content: Jess Yugovich is among the Australians trying MDMA-assisted psychotherapy after being diagnosed with PTSD, as highly regulated facilities process the drug for therapeutic use.

items.title: Bringing Them Home inaction a 'woeful failure', ex-commissioner says

children.content: Mick Dodson heard "soul-destroying" testimony from more than 500 First Nations people during a landmark inquiry. He says that 29 years later, it's time for action.

image.altText: A man, wearing a tshirt and an akubra hat, stands outside in front of trees.

items.title: Brad Scott sacked as Essendon coach after one win in past 24 matches

children.content: Essendon sacks head coach Brad Scott as the Bombers languish at the bottom of the AFL ladder.

image.altText: AFL coach looking annoyed as he walks off the ground

items.title: 'Catastrophic failure' after drones malfunction at Vivid Sydney show

children.content: The incident occurred not long into Monday's 7.30pm "star-bound" show when almost 90 drones began falling out of formation and into the harbour around Cockle Bay.

image.altText: Drones carrying colourful lights form an unidentifiable shape over a bay at night

gallery.altText: Drone lights falling into the water below, a skyscraper in the background.

items.title: One world record and a whole lot of regret for Enhanced Games athletes

image.altText: Kristian Gkolomeev,holds up two trophies

children.content: Despite the drugs and the super suits, the Enhanced Games delivered little more than a smattering of PBs and a single world fastest time for the hefty price of an athlete's hard-earned credibility.

items.title: Missing man's ex-girlfriend feared being 'jumped'

children.content: The former girlfriend of missing Queensland man Jack McLennan says she held fears for her safety because of rumours surrounding his disappearance.

image.altText: Jack McLennan and Carlee Beetham pictured happy together in front of a waterfall

items.title: 'My time to go': The words that showed Daniher's humanity in tragedy

children.content: Neale Daniher showed leadership on and off the field throughout his life, from club captaincy to coaching and as Australian of the Year — but no more so when facing the challenge of an MND diagnosis.

image.altText: GettyImages-971482788

items.title: Richard Gadd 'couldn't escape' the story he wanted to tell after Baby Reindeer

children.content: After Baby Reindeer — about a comedian and his stalker — became a surprise hit, Richard Gadd ignored the pressure to replicate his success, making the show he couldn't stop thinking about.

gallery.altText: A TV still of Richard Gadd, 37, with a beard and short back and sides, in a leather jacket, eyeballing the camera.

items.title: The many phases of Miles Davis, who would have turned 100 today

children.content: From Illinois to New York to Australia, legendary trumpeter Miles Davis helped shape the direction of jazz with albums like Birth of the Cool and Kind of Blue.

image.altText: A black and white photo of Miles Davis playing the trumpet. His eyes look down and he has a serious expression.

items.title: Using AI to manage your budget? This is what experts want you to know

children.content: "Hallucinations", privacy and other factors to consider if you're turning to artificial intelligence for financial advice.

items.title: What do plummeting clearance rates mean for the property market?

children.content: Early signs of an Australian housing market correction are now clearly visible, and at the heart of the recent property market weakness is less vendor power.

items.title: Socceroos dealt World Cup injury blow with star midfielder ruled out

children.content: The Socceroos have been dealt a massive injury blow ahead of next month's FIFA World Cup, with midfielder Riley McGree ruled out of the tournament.

items.title: NACC chief Brereton to face Senate grilling after shock resignation

children.content: National Anti-Corruption Commissioner Paul Brereton will face a grilling by senators at estimates hearings today following his shock resignation on Monday, amid calls for a wider reset of the troubled agency.

image.altText: Paul Brereton speaks during a committee hearing.

children.content: Social housing tenants in inner Sydney have repeatedly complained about illegal squatters, they have been removed by police but they keep coming back.

children.content: It comes as health facilities in some of the worst-hit regions are attacked amid ongoing violence.

children.content: Pope Leo XIV urges global governments to slow down the development of AI systems and their disruptive effects.

items.title: 'Inhumane' disability home allegedly had fridges locked as residents slept on floor

children.content: Former workers at a disability home in Western Australia's far north raise allegations of neglect, including medical mismanagement, locked fridges and a child being placed at risk.

image.altText: a house and a leafy front yard

items.title: Australian family demands action one year after son's Bali death

children.content: A year after their 23-year-old son died in Bali in "unclear" circumstances, a Queensland couple have unanswered questions.

image.altText: A 23-year-old man smiling at the camera.

items.title: Flexible work broader than just working from home

children.content: A bill amending the Fair Work Act 2009 to include the right to work from home is too narrow in its focus and could lead to discrimination, according to submissions in a federal inquiry.

gallery.altText: A woman sitting at her desk working from home.

thumbnail.altText: A woman sitting at her desk working from home.

items.title: Kokkinakis wins French Open five-set thriller on mixed day for Aussies

children.content: Thanasi Kokkinakis has roared back from the brink of defeat after four-and-a-quarter hours in sweltering Paris heat to launch another trademark five-set comeback at the French Open.

image.altText: Male tennis player serving in front of a crowd

items.title: Super Netball record broken by 16yo school captain Eva Carneiro

children.content: The Giants team is flailing at the bottom of the ladder, winless after 11 rounds and the likely recipient of this year's wooden spoon. But their brand new debutant Eva Carneiro has brought some much needed excitement for fans, as the teenager breaks the record for the youngest-ever player in Super Netball.

image.altText: Two players smile and one points their fingers

items.title: Community 'devastated' by plans for gas-powered data centres

children.content: More than 200 people voice their opposition to plans to build one of the country’s largest gas-fired power plants in the NSW Southern Highlands to provide energy for data centres.

image.altText: a line of people stand in rain gear on a field a child in the foreground hangs over a 'stop gas' sign

items.title: Call for island off Brisbane to introduce public bus

children.content: Macleay Island residents say the community has a "very high percentage" of people who are elderly and have a disability, who would benefit from a bus service.

image.altText: Three women standing on the side of a street. They are on at the bottom of the hill.

children.content: Police believe changes to speed limits on country roads in Victoria have led to vandalism including new road signs being cut down.

items.title: Push to find resting places for remains found in desecrated graves

children.content: Nine piles of dirt containing up to 5,000 human remains from dug-up graves have been dumped at the rear of a regional Victorian cemetery. A team of archaeologists is helping to find a home for the disturbed remains.
